questions.question: Find the crosswind component when landing on runway 22 if ATC says winds are 150° at 17 knots. Variation is 9° W.
The Cessna 208 has a maximum demonstrated crosswind of 20 kts. What is the strongest wind you can have if you are landing on runway 4 and the wind is from 350°?
How much crosswind do you have under the following conditions: Winds aloft 230° at 17 knots, true course 282°, true airspeed 112 knots?
You are departing runway 32. If the winds are 337@16, what is the crosswind component?

Your turbofan engine has a starting limitation of no more than 18 knots of tailwind. If your heading indicator shows 165° and the surface winds are from 020°, what is the highest wind speed that would be within your limit?
What will the crosswind component be on landing?
questions.context: Use the following information for the remaining 5 questions. The AWOS broadcasts winds 150° at 24 KTS with a temperature of +9°C, Altimeter 29.79" Hg. The fix "NIFAA" is 11.2 nm from the threshold of runway 18. Runway 18 has a magnetic course of 177° with a variation of 5° E and is located at 1,241' MSL. The weather is the same at all altitudes.

questions.question: What is the crosswind component for takeoff under the following conditions? You depart from runway 11, ATIS reports winds 160° at 17 knots. Magnetic variation is 8° E.
You have decided to limit yourself to 10 knots of crosswind. If the winds are 350° at 22 knots and you are departing runway 31, would you be within your limits?

choices.text: No, it is a 14 knot crosswind
No, it is a 17 knot crosswind
No, it is an 11 knot crosswind
Yes, it is a 7 knot crosswind

questions.correctChoice: No, it is a 14 knot crosswind

questions.question: Tuning in the ASOS, you hear current winds of 230° at 17 KTS, gusting to 29 KTS. What is the steady headwind component on runway 18L?
The current winds at KOSH are 240° at 21 knots. If you intend to land on runway 27, what is the crosswind component?

The MD-11 normally has a maximum takeoff weight of 630,500 pounds. This number has to be reduced by approximately 30,000 pounds for each 10 knots of crosswind when departing from an un-grooved, wet runway up to a maximum crosswind of 24 knots. You're planning a flight from Taipei, Taiwan to Anchorage, Alaska departing on runway 5R at Taipei and the winds are reported as 100° at 28 knots and the runways are soaked thanks to a recent rainstorm. The ramp agent at Taipei is asking for the maximum possible payload. If your basic operating weight (including flight crew) is 257,511 pounds and the fuel load necessary to operate the flight with required reserves, alternate, etc is 197,200 pounds, what will you tell the ramp agent is the maximum payload for today's flight? (Choose the closest figure.)

choices.text: None of the above, the crosswind component exceeds 24 knots

questions.question: You're a newly minted FedEx B767 captain based in Hong Kong subject to higher weather minimums until obtaining 100 hours on the aircraft. To be a suitable alternate airport, the lowest reported values can be a 300-foot ceiling and 1 statute mile visibility. FedEx B767 aircraft are approved for land with up to a 15-kt tailwind, 15-kt crosswind and/or 25-kt headwind. Based on the following weather forecast and airport information, can Chengdu, China (Runway Configuration: 2L/20R and 2R/20L) be planned as an alternate for your upcoming flight? [[NEXT_LINE]] Wind: 350° at 13 meters/second [[NEXT_LINE]] Visibility: 1500 meters
You are in Sun Valley, ID (5320') and the METAR reports winds are 270° at 32 knots. Your aircraft limitation is a 10-knot tailwind. Can you accept a departure off runway 15? (variation 13° E).
What is the headwind component for Runway 16 with winds 122° at 14 knots?

The maximum allowable cross wind component for takeoff for your aircraft is 35 knots, including gusts. The maximum allowable tailwind is 10 knots. If the winds are from 180° at 35 knots, with gusts to 42 knots, which of the following runways is permitted for takeoff?

choices.text: None of these runways are legal. You'll need to wait for the weather to improve.

questions.correctChoice: None of these runways are legal. You'll need to wait for the weather to improve.

questions.question: Tuning in the ATIS, you hear winds of 300° at 13 knots, gusting to 21 knots. What is the steady headwind component on runway 35R?
The ATIS reports the wind from 330° at 22 knots. You are taking off from runway 26. What is the crosswind component?
The maximum allowable cross wind component for your aircraft is 35 knots, including gusts. Which of the following winds will allow you to take off from runway 34R?
Winds at the field are 240 @ 52 KTS. What is the crosswind component when landing on runway 27?
At what angle to an aircraft's course is the wind blowing if winds are 210° at 18 KTS and TAS is 103 KTS? The crosswind component of the wind is 11 KTS.
Runway 13 has reported wind of 180 at 25 KTS. What is the headwind component?
You are departing runway 25R and the winds are 300° at 24 knots. Does this exceed the maximum demonstrated crosswind limitation of 15 knots?

choices.text: No. It is right at the limitation.
No. It is four knots under the limitation.
Yes. You exceed the limitation by three knots.
Yes. You exceed the limitation by eight knots.

questions.correctChoice: Yes. You exceed the limitation by three knots.

questions.question: How many knots of crosswind does an aircraft experience on final for runway 2? Winds are 330° at 28 knots. Aircraft true airspeed is 160 mph.
Surface winds from the last METAR were reported to be 350@23 knots You plan to takeoff runway 5. If the variation at the field is 16° east, what is your crosswind component?
